notes: Close & redeem now moves REAL money (Option A). Closing an investment makes the actual savings account balance go UP by the redeemed amount (the Journal/dashboard savings tile changes, not just the projection), via a genuine redemption transaction. The close lifecycle EVENT now only stops future contributions and zeroes the instrument's projected balance from the close month — it no longer emits a separate projection redemption (which previously double-counted). The single money-mover is the transaction: applyTransactionDelta credits savings.openingBalance by the redeemed amount (so the tile rises) but deliberately does NOT reduce the investment's horizon-start seed for close redemptions (the close event already zeroes the projection forward; touching the seed would corrupt past months like May). All savings views were reconciled to count the real redemption: the engine savings overlay and reverse-walk, the projection sheet's planned-savings row (via the income-folded surplus), and the Net Worth savings chain (projectSavingsChain now adds current/future redemption transactions landing in savings). Net effect for closing RD in June: RD shows its accumulated value through May and 0 from June; the actual savings account tile increases by the redeemed balance; a redemption entry appears in the Journal and the instrument's history; projection and Net Worth savings both step up consistently — with no double-count.
